reviews.review_title: Too pricy for the amount of content
reviews.review_description: So bassicaly this was the full price when I bought it, no discount the full price it is. And I think there is not enough content for this price, I also think the design, cutscenes and writing are great but not the characters(they are probably lore in the comics I don't read) but overall 3 stars(mostly because of the price)

I have seen some good VR games trough the years but this game is definitely on top of my list. It is well made, polished, interesting and funny. The Ghost Rider part was amazing. The bosses are sooo cool, and the game is a mix of Doom, Quake, Duke Nukem, and 8 bit arcade. You can tell that this game was made by some old school gamers. It even has a reference to the first "Speed" movie with Sandra Bullock and Keanu Reeves which is soo cool. I really hope we could see more titles made by gamers for gamers. 10/10

There are definitely some things that could have been better, but it’s overall an amazing game, and I would highly recommend playing this. There is a pretty good amount of replayability, especially for completionists like me, but I think $50 is a lot of money for a game like this. I was really excited for Deadpool VR, and I’m also excited to play games like this in the future.

The graphics are good, it has a good number of enemies and bosses, and its campaign has a decent size. However, giving its price, I was expecting something on the same level of Batman, or even Iron Man, but this game is not as good as those. The graphics are good, but the levels feel generic, and there is nothing that really stands out (other stylized games like Jurassic World Aftermath had environments that felt more interesting). The combat gets old fast, and it reminds me of old arcade VR games. It's basically a Doom VFR style of gameplay, but with worse looking enemies and without the cool big guns. The arena that you play between each level is also repetitive and boring, and the game suffers from unskippable scenes with NPCs talking for minutes that are a chore to get through. Overall, if you like these types of games where you hipfire into anything that moves while you keep jumping to avoid enemy attacks, then it's an ok game since at least it has a proper campaign, but I would recommend getting on a sale because I think the game is way too expensive for what it delivers. If you are expecting a game on the same level as the other Quest exclusives, then I think you will be  disappointed.

i really loved the game but i had thought that they make a 2nd vr game about Deadpool so im leaving this review to tell you guys to make a 2nd game not begging but if you do make it with Deadpool spiral all the different Deadpools mojo Deadpool's space friend forgot his name and that's it thank you! Edit: this came out on my birthday

I wish you had limited ammo, swords didn't keep re appearing in you back pocket or a limited amount of grenades. Its not Batman Arkam VR but with the current state of vr, its still a experience worth experiencing.

reviews.review_description: This game is beautiful in every way possible because the developers are awesome. But meta and Zuckerberg decided they don't want to invest anymore money in this studio, or any others because VR is not profitable for them I guess. Now they're more interested in wearables. This studio is so talented that this game is stunningly beautiful, funny, intriguing, and graphically incredible. That's what you get when you have amazing Developers. Also last thing is that he talks about many inappropriate things that many kids will find funny but it’s not. This could affect their behavior. I’m 12 but i know that his humor is inappropriate. Many people will not.
The game was so good graphics were good best game for deadpool fans like me I recommend this game for any one
Took me a few couple months to finish and it's absolute cinema as a deadpool fan I say this is amazing

I’m a huge Deadpool fan, and unfortunately, I got way too hyped up about this game. When I found out it would be replayable with different characters and a new set of weapons, I was convinced I’d complete all the side activities 100%. Unfortunately, I was wrong. The game is great on the first playthrough. Unfortunately, the inability to skip dialogue lines makes it a chore. I try to understand this approach, since the developers probably wanted us to hear all the new dialogue lines, but they didn’t create any new lines for the NPCs, only for the different versions of Deadpool - and even though they’re really great and funny, hearing the same NPCs over and over again is just exhausting.

The gameplay itself is packed with action, and the story is told in a pretty interesting way. Performance is okay if you play with QGO.

Game is good and all but $50 come on that like mad expensive for some game that’s just as good as a $20 game
Also why’d u guys fire all your employees

BTW I am all up to date with my software updates. Even Worse though is that meta laid off the studio that made this game so all of the bugs will likely never get fixed.

Gameplay okay 

Story ahh 

After the first mission its ahh￼

🚨 Full video review available by searching ‘Gamertag VR’ on YouTube🚨

Marvels Deadpool is a vr brawler made up of several chapters, some epic cinematic cut pieces which oddly can be skipped, 5 boss battles and 4 random arena fights which I’ll explain more about in a minute. 

The combat is all about mixing up melee and weapon based attacks sometimes for combos to charge a special meter and voilence to hype your mojo stream viewers using a huge arsenal of guns and swords which have to be unlocked using Mojo coins (more on what the mojo stuff means in a minute) and there’s lots of weapons with some that are level specific, far example when your fighting waves of zombies made up of Nova core centurions you’ll get some chargeable blasters or later on on ultra reds level you’ll get spinnaker sword blades you. Deadpool vr is all about fast movement, fast reactions, minor platforming and trying not get your limbs cut off which take sometime to grow back but it never gets old to see and your job is to simply fight your way to the end boss. 

Theres no open worlds segments and it’s a very straight forward A to B level design. Theres Deadpool comic books to find some of which will require some exploration but nothing major, and there’s some very cool marvel cameos, especially when you fight alongside the epic ghost rider. 

I want to add Marvel Deadpool is very much not what you would expect of a meta exclusive. It’s got tons of bad language and lots of violence seeing you cut enemies in half or literally in bits and it does a decent job of being what you would expect a Deadpool game to be and I’m glad in this regard it doesn’t hold back. 

To note there is a kid friendly mode in the options which I personally have not tried for this review, but I would assume it would remove all the gore and swearing and there’s a steamer mode which removes all the licenced music which kicks in when you build up to use your super meter which gives you limited access to some special weapons either at random or by choice like thors hammer or a deck of gambits explosively charged cards. 

There also some epic cinematic parts that I mentioned earlier and your offered to skip these because they are quite full on in terms of motion. but I would definitely not skip these if you’ve never had motion sickness in vr as they can be a very memorable part of the game.
Game is awesome, only bad thing is it is just a bunch of mini games really. Still fun and is a must buy

reviews.review_title: JESUS CHRIST I LOVE THIS GAME
reviews.review_description: So I had been waiting for this game to release and when it did, I instantly bought it. I cannot express how much I love this game, although Meta laid off Twisted Pixel (just learned about it today). I enjoyed playing their previous game, Path of The Warrior and this game had the same elements as that one, I love DP with all my heart. Deadpools jokes and seemingly randomly-generated personality just stuck with me ever since I first watched Deadpool. The mechanics and different dialogue with each of the different characters actually really surprised me. I'm just really hoping there will be more Deadpool games like this one.

When I read the negative reviews of the "so many cutscenes, not enough playtime", I nearly didn't purchase the game, as well as people saying the "combat is lacking"

If you're a Deadpool fan, and think the negative reviews make the game feel like one of those "fake vr" games where interactions are mostly just buttons and not so much interaction

That is how I felt when reading the reviews, but as I purchased and played, I am at the 3rd mission (About 3 hours in), and I have had many laughs and a lot of fun

If you're expecting the combat to be gory and super duper realistic, get out of here, this is Deadpool, expect comic book combat with dismemberment enabled

It's fun, it's a laugh, it's Deadpool, the negative reviews feel like false people that only like Deadpool for the action and not what Deadpool actually is as a character

If you like Deadpool as a comic rather than as a action thriller romance, then this game is for you, if you think of Deadpool as  an annoying psychopathic killer with a cool fighting style, this game is half for you.. if you think of Deadpool as a realistic combat fighter with mutation powers... You are looking for Blade & Sorcery, not Deadpool
But really think about it Marvel Deadpool VR is this good imagine how good marvel spider man VR would be.

Tons of great moments and excellent, satisfying, fast paced gameplay. The game does a great job of capturing how fun it is to be Deadpool. I've also yet to encounter any game breaking bugs. 

Now the negatives: do NOT spend $50 on this game. Lots of repetition. Busy work for busy work's sake. A few too many mini games. And the exposition. My god, the exposition. There are unskippable 15 minute scenes where you legitimately can do nothing🤷 if you only have an hour to spare, play a different game, because you spend entirely too much time in this game standing there waiting for the action to start. Wanting more action in a Deadpool game is not unreasonable. 

Overall, lots of fun and definitely well made and worth playing! But it's tough to justify that pricetag.

If you like fun, action packed game with a comic like look, this game is for you. The game has a lot of swearing, and inappropriate jokes, but there is a nice friendly version in settings called kid pool, it removes all bad language and bad jokes, the gore (blood) is replaced with confetti. This game is definitely one of my top favorite games to play. A really cool thing about the game is there are these comic books to collect throughout the story, reminds me of the gold bricks from lego games. There are also different deadpool skins to choose from for replaying the story mode, including weapons as well. The game has rooms that can only be unlocked after gameplay of story is finished. One other thing is that I love how deadpool talks about the player as in you and makes so many references to other movies, shows, characters, and more. But yeah, if you like deadpool, and want a fun actioned story to follow as him, Id say that this game is absolutely worth $50, even though thats kinda high. But thats how much games will cost now.

reviews.review_title: "Please sir, can I have some more"
reviews.review_description:
Getting hopelessly obsessed with this game and playing through it in a few days was like going to a beautiful restaurant, getting this delicious gourmet dish, and then finding out that dish was the only thing on the menu

Where's my side? Where's my dessert? This was a roller coaster ride I didn't want to end!

The combat is so fun and exciting, it leaves a lot of room for player experimentation, and Deadpool's dialogue is very similar to how he was portrayed in the movie trilogy with the crass humor, sarcasm, and fourth wall breaks

Would've been 5 stars if there was a horde mode or something similar, something that can be played casually where there's no dialogue to get in the way 'cause I just wanna fight people

So in conclusion: Gameplay good, story is fine, Deadpool is very Deadpool and the new voice actor did a great job, I just wish there was more
At the end of the game please add a bonus level that you fight the Deadpool core

This game makes you feel like Deadpool the jokes are funny it made me laugh a lot and the game is beautiful and there are some amazing cameos in this game and after it ended I already wanted more  it was awesome it’s my favorite VR game✨

reviews.review_title: you’d mostly enjoy if Deadpool fan
reviews.review_description: So glad the crude humor of Deadpool was not censored as i feared it would be. So far, the action is just non stop overwhelming action and bombastic explosions! Dunno if just me but the accuracy of the pistols feels “off” and disappointedly feels light weight for a high price game of this caliber. The comic book graphics are okay but bit disappointing for a game that’s $50. But I guess makes sense since this involves a comic book related character. The gore isn’t as vivid as I imagined it to be. Anyhows, no bugs experienced so far and very enjoyable! Highly recommend!

I completed the game once and now it won't let me play the game on a different save file which is a shame because the game itself was really good and made me laugh multiple times definitely needs a bug fix.

BORING. Complete style over substance. A pile of great IP, characters, art, music, great performance by Neil Patrick Harris, that together is just a mess.

Even on the highest difficulty on the first time though I was yawning mid-combat. Dying feels irrelevant as you just instantly pick up exactly where you left off. Combat is a wave- your-arms-around affair. Getting better at it looks possible, but why? There are so many things to try to make it fun all dancing around the fact it's just NOT. No amount of collectibles, skins, or cameos is ever going to make it interesting. I can't imagine EVER wanting to circle back any reply to unlock any of the things you can unlock because the first time though I was just trying to get though to see if it was going anywhere. 

Enemy AI is utterly stupid, and the LOOONG cut scenes, puzzles that are literally what you'd give a baby to shut them up. 

I played this and Batman Arkham Shadow in the same month and oh my lord there's no comparison. 

If you're going to laugh and smile literally because it's got MCU characters and retro music, go ahead, but if you're looking for an actual game, DO NOT BUT THIS!
I was playing and 2 minutes in and i loved it best game and the jokes and best story line ever

reviews.review_title: I wish the studio wasn't laid off
reviews.review_description: So, since I learned that Twisted pixel was laid off, I was really upset because it would mean they couldn't add Custom settings. An example would be that what if you don't like bad words but love the violence (I am not weird) this would help because I'd like the violence without bad words so my parents would be okay with this because the confetti/colorful blood does get annoying most times. So, I'd say this would've been a good suggestion.
PEAK!

Be aware, there is a horrible bug with the game. It’s something with the tracking, the screen goes black and white and it freezes and it’s almost game  braking at times. This is something the developers need to get fixed ASAP. When it does, it’s a 4.6⭐️  and I’ll change my rating to 5( 4.6) . 

EDIT The developer says this is intended to keep the player inside the boundaries, but I am well within my play boundaries. I’m fortunate to have a very large space so maybe it’s too sensitive or it’s a tracking issue with the Quest.

REVIEW-
For the last three years, Meta has consistently launched a first party flagship, high quality VR game, which is very much appreciated. This game picks up where Arkham shadow and Asgard‘s wrath 2 left off and continues the tradition with a high production quality and lengthy game. The studios Meta has acquired have yet to disappoint and their releases have become a highlight of the VR gaming year for myself who is a PCVR gamer. Some of the reviews I.e Gamer￼ Tag and Mateo 311 had some critiques about this game and some of them are justified, but then I also can’t help but feel that they are rushing through these games to get their review out first and that’s exactly what it feels like when watching their reviews. The race for them to get clicks and views creates a conflict of interest so I would say watch as many reviews as you can. IGN who I would normally never recommend actually has a very fair review on this gam so does Caboose who typically covers flatscreen games. A couple of my critiques are- 

1) there is no collision detection at all 

2) outside of going for style and trying to get creative there isn’t a lot of depth to the combat.
. 
That being said if you were looking for a fun game with a good story with a lengthy campaign and a high production value this is it and it should be on most people’s radar. I’ve seen a couple complain about performance and low frame rate and that’s got to be something to do with their headset or the space available because performance was very good with the exception of the bug and I would say performance was better better than Arkham shadow on release day.

developer_response.body: VRoniin, sorry you're having this experience in the game. This is not a bug in the game. This is intended hardware design for when a players steps outside of their playspace boundary. I would suggest trying to increase your playspace sizing to see if that helps the issue not happen as often.

reviews.review_description:
🚨Gameplay of the full levels is on my YouTube under the name "VR Central" and a full review is under the secondary channel, "Face Toaster"🚨
The game has moutains of dialogue, plenty of cosmetics, and has an interesting way of expanding existing content with letting you replay the games with Deadpool Variants.
Its fantastic if you are a big deadpool fan. You'll love this if you love watching Deadpool, mainly to hear his quips.
I do wish that there were more levels instead of having these "mini worlds" in a fighting arena staggered between the larger worlds. And it does feel like there could have been a larger variety in the types of firearms rather than changing stats on the existing firearms. The biggest irritating part with the game though is if you want to go back and experience a particular part, there is no way to fast-forward or skip any parts. If you want to JUST experience the boss battles again, you have to go back through the ENTIRE level. Even the slow cutscenes that you already know everything that is going to be said.

All-in-all it is a fantastic game that I love but wish that there could be more available "skips" and weapon variety to increase the replayability. Overall a fantastic game if you love deadpool!

This game was exceptionally  great with the fun cartoony theme but there is a lot of violence in this game also they nearly made  more mini games  then actual levels but I still think this is a great vr game thanks for reading this I hope It helped

reviews.review_description: Marvel's Deadpool VR leaves you wanting more from this version of Wade Wilson with it's humor and hack'n slash action even with a less polished final challenge.

Game-breaking bug
Moon level blue area: enemies spawn behind doors in unloaded space and run into invisible walls, blocking progression.
Restarting app, headset, reinstalling, and restarting levels does not fix it.i wish i could play but i cant progress.

I enjoy this game a lot, everything from the story to the humor, the action is great as well, I am at the part where they capture Ultimo, after work today I come home and the game will not load, it will sit at the loading screen until it says PILO not responding. I uninstall my game and redownload that didn't help, I shut off my headset and restarted, nothing. I do not understand what is going on as it worked flawlessly the past 2 days

developer_response.body:
I'm very sorry this is happening to you. Please try resetting your headset and then try launching the game again. Also verify your Headset OS is on the latest version (older than V79 will have issues)
If you post this in the Meta Quest Discord we would be happy to help troubleshoot this issue with you.

reviews.review_title: I can’t even download the game
reviews.review_description: I bought the game and I beat the whole thing and then i deleted it for this other game and that got boring so I tried to download the Deadpool game again and it keeps getting stuck

It’s a decent game that looks great and it made me laugh, but it’s very repetitive and full of bugs. I can’t play more than an hour without the game crashing or freezing. The story has almost no plot either…just a series of fetch missions and mini games with at least half of the game being these really lame and boring “arena battles” that were so stupid and annoying that I just wanted to get through them as quickly as possible to get back to some (admittedly fairly fun) violence in the actual levels. I don’t know what the devs were thinking, making the entire plot revolve around Mojo and his ridiculous Mojo-world. There is SO MUCH they could’ve done with a VR Deadpool game and instead they chose to turn it into inter-dimensional TikTok/reality TV??!! What a let down! I actually would’ve preferred a lazy rehash of the movies over this…and I hate when games do that. There’s no way that the price is justified either. Maybe if they actually got Ryan Reynolds to voice DP, but even then, I’d assume that most of the purchase price would be going towards his pay, because there’s no way they spent enough making the actual game to justify that price   . This is basically a $30CAN game that’s been cynically overpriced to more than double that. But then, what did I expect from a collab between Meta and Marvel…two of the greediest corporations to ever exist. I really wish I had listened to that little voice in my head telling me not to keep playing past the 2 hour mark so I could get a refund. Instead I’m stuck with a game that I played for 5 hours or so and will likely never play again.

I have been a Deadpool fan for years now and when I saw this game was releasing I was SO hyped!and it lived up to its hype. one minor thing was my game was stuck loading for a minute so I reset the game and it worked again but this is awesome.also love ya wolvey
This game is amazing in so many ways, but I have to admit that the minigames were the only part I didn’t fully enjoy. Everything else is fantastic, so it’s very sad to see the studio gone.

This game is very good but there are some issues. There are a lot of tracking issues and glitches and it’s not the controllers. One of them is where his arms go insane and it’s hard to do anything and you have to open your menu and close it to work again. And there are some more that I won’t discuss. But Deadpool is really funny cracking jokes some inappropriate ones and 4th wall breaks. But if you are younger and like Deadpool and you get this game be careful because they curse way more than the movies. But overall I’d rate this game an 8/10 but if they fix the issues it’d be a 9 since it’s annoying how much they curse. Not at all like the movies.


Edit- I just beat the game and discovered some more issues. I discovered way more tracking issues and head placement is one of them its when you spawn in a mission and your head is way above your body and it breaks the game and I don’t know how to fix it.  There is also issues with the game crashing occasionally when your literally doing nothing and I don’t think this game is worth 50 bucks but the game has a good story and funny jokes but I would recommend waiting for the game to go on sale.



Another edit- I’ve just beat the game again and it is still the same as the last time I beat it but they did fix the issues besides the head placement in the new update but I discovered if you reset your position with your meta button on your right controller it’s fixed but sometimes your head still could be a little bit above your body but that is fine and you can still play normally but I also found out I kept complaining about all the cursing but there is actually a “kid pool” mode and it bleeps out the curse words and that is a plus for me since  there are some little kids who might play this game but the only bad part about the kid pool mode is that it makes the blood confetti and it can break the game sometimes like when I started the story again a couple days ago I tried the kid pool mode out and it’s neat but after the intro where you get used to shooting your guns by shooting what Deadpool calls “goons” in moving cars you are in a hellacarrier and when I got at that part I started shooting people to progress and confetti was all over my screen and the sound started glitching and my game crashed and it didn’t load for a day so I won’t be using that again.
The guns are great and so are the hand gestures I love funny dialogue

reviews.review_title: Fun if you make it fun, but gets boring
reviews.review_description:
The game starts a strong start, trying new weapons and taking enemies in a creative way, however. The combat is fun if you try to make it fun, meaning get creative with your takedowns. But even with that, the game gets so boring, in the last few hours i literally tried to finish the game and speedrun it, because it gets repetitive and repetitive and repetitive, same style of enemies, no variation, nothing at all, and in terms of dialogue and cutscenes, they sometimes make you laugh but don’t expect deadpool movie humor here, most of them won’t make you smile. But some will make you laugh, the bosses don’t challenge you and same as the enemies it really gets repetitive. If I can describe this game at best. Is repetition, and to be honest even the AI is really stupid in this game so even if you try to make it fun. It won’t be as fun as you intentionally make it. 

Overall. This game starts well, but there is a lot of dialogue and cutscenes, which isn’t a problem. Its just that they didn’t dk well, also the combat is so boring and repetitive. Even the same finishers, its sad because they had a lot of potential with the a combat with a charecter like deadpool, they couldve had a skill tree levels or something, upgrades, etc. or different types of enemies with different ways to take them down. Make the bosses more challenging, to sum it up. This game is a swallowable disspointment.

I spent the first hour in Marvel’s Deadpool VR and this is the best VR game of 2025 alongside with Reach, this mechanics and gameplay are super fun and the boss fights are amazing, this game hits the spots of comedy and Neil Patrick Harris does an amazing Deadpool, The weapons are super fun and accessible and there are many ways to kill enemies, such as ripping off your hand and throwing it at them, fling them off the ground with the grapple gun, shoot them and stab them in anyway that you would like, This game is a 9/10 and I can’t wait for more games in the future. PEAK

reviews.review_title: Good if he sounded like Deadpool
reviews.review_description:
I’m not sure what direction they were looking for in the voice actor for deadpool, but for me its just not it, but is CALMLY saying his lines while the ENEMIES sound better

Not sure if they couldn’t afford Nolan North to VA or looking for a Ryan Reynolds soundalike, but dude has no energy behind the lines for how Deadpool usually acts

Besides that, stabbing and shooting enemies is fun when there’s more than a few ways to kill ways to chop baddies
This game won’t run anymore for whatever reason. Tried everything and just won’t start the game where I left it, kicks me back to the main screen. Don’t buy it!
This game was a blast so far I think if the studio did not shut down I think this game could have had much more potential 🤔

reviews.review_title: This game needs an Oscar award
reviews.review_description: !WARNING REVIEW CONTAINS SPOILERS!                                                                                                                                  You jump in and you get THE MOST CINEMATIC INTRO OF YOUR LIFE! Then you get to fight a boss along side incredible humor which makes the game just so much fun to play. After that you get to fight in Japan, a casino, space, and Russia (I think) and get to play with MECHS!? Then have fun fighting all those villains again for a rematch. Then you get to fight the champion and for the surprise ending… YOU REALLY THOUGHT I WAS GONNA SPOIL THE WHOLE GAME INCLUDING THE INSANE ENDING!?
